We cover the latest news, share our thoughts on new episodes, make predictions, discuss spoilers, and have fun along the way. If you love Odyssey as much as we do, you're in the right place.

title: OnTheBallBets: UK Betting Insights - UK Bookmaker Reviews, Betting Tips & Bonus Analysis
author: Mike Lovatt
description:
OnTheBallBets: UK Betting Insights is your essential guide to UK sports betting, bookmaker reviews, and bonus analysis. 
Hosted by Mike Lovatt, owner of OnTheBallBets, this podcast provides independent analysis of UK bookmaker offers, betting strategies, and industry insights. Each episode covers bookmaker comparisons, free bet evaluations, welcome bonus breakdowns, and betting tips for major sporting events.
Whether you're comparing bet365 bonus code vs Betfred's sign up offer, understanding wagering requirements, or looking for Cheltenham betting tips, OnTheBallBets delivers transparent recommendations for UK punters.
Topics covered:
- UK bookmaker reviews and ratings
- Free bet and welcome bonus analysis
- Betting tips for football, horse racing, and major sporting events
- Bookmaker feature comparisons (cash out, bet builder, live streaming)
- Wagering requirements and terms explained
- Payment method guides (PayPal betting, instant withdrawals)
- Betting strategy and money management
New episodes weekly. Visit https://www.ontheballbets.com for detailed bookmaker comparisons and ratings.
18+ only. BeGambleAware.org.

title: Kingdom Living Now
author: Brian Reese
description: Building believers one level at a time, helping them grow, expand and be the one of Gods best, so we can help others.

title: Raymond Report Audio Files
author: Ron Raymond
description: Free daily MLB previews in Ron Raymond's voice — the line, the total, the pitchers and the lean. Get the full slate at ATSStats.com.

title: Serenity Sessions
author: Ashleigh
description:
Welcome to the Serenity Sessions.

We're Ashleigh and Tanya, two hairdressers who met through a shared passion for helping people experiencing hair loss and a desire to make a positive change in the industry.

Each episode is an honest, down-to-earth conversation covering everything from scalp health, hair loss and non-surgical hair replacement to life behind the salon chair, running businesses, developing products and navigating an ever-evolving industry.

We'll be busting myths, sharing real client experiences, talking about the highs and lows of business, and giving you an authentic behind-the-scenes look at what really goes on in the world of hair loss.

Whether you're a hair professional, someone experiencing hair loss, or simply curious to learn more, pull up a chair and join the conversation.

Just honest conversations, expert knowledge and a genuine passion for making scalp health simple.

title: Cabinetry Accelerator
author: Travis Scharn
description:
The Cabinetry Accelerator Podcast is the go-to show for cabinet shop owners who want to grow faster, work smarter, and build a more profitable business.
Designed to complement the Cabinetry Accelerator Program, this podcast dives deep into the real-world strategies behind scaling a cabinetry business, from increasing production efficiency and pricing for profit to building systems, hiring the right team, and stepping out of day-to-day chaos.
Each episode delivers practical insights, proven frameworks, and honest conversations tailored specifically to custom and production cabinetry businesses.

title: You Are My Destiny Radio Show episode 1 with Luca & Claudette, special guest Joseph Wooten
author: Zanna Records
description:
You Are My Destiny Radio Show — Episode 1, with Luca and Claudette. Today, we talk about one of the most powerful forces in the universe: the human mind, and its extraordinary ability to turn dreams into reality. We also take you behind the scenes of our music video collaboration with 3-time Grammy-nominated keyboardist Joseph Wooten — filmed in the music capital of the world, Nashville, Tennessee.
www.ZannaRecordsRadio.com www.LucaClaudette.com

title: Key Decisions Podcast
author: Keith Sothern & Olwyn Lydon
description:
Hosted by Keith Sothern & Olwyn Lydon, from REA Sothern Real Estate Agents.


We’ve decided to jump into the podcast world because, to be honest, property talk can be a bit of a head-melter. Each episode, Last Mile sits down with leading policymakers, technologists, philanthropists, and innovators in tech to discover how an abundance mindset is transforming the talent pipeline in the innovation economy.  

Last Mile Education Fund provides direct, no-strings-attached gifts to STEM students who are financially vulnerable and within reach of their degrees–students who are brilliant, motivated, and a few hundred dollars away from a future that transforms not just their lives, but the industries that need them. 

This podcast explores the imperative–and the opportunity–to build abundance into systems that were designed for scarcity.

From Fortune 500 leaders and nonprofit founders to White House alumni and first-generation college alumni who are redefining tech, these conversations highlight class diversity as a competitive advantage for innovators. 

Abundance Imperative is for donors who want to invest in the next generation, for industry leaders who want to find undiscovered talent in new places, and for Last Mile students who want to see what their future holds.

title: Unusual Apocalypse
author: HavenMedia
description:
"Is this thing still on? Testing, one, two... If you can hear my voice over the sound of screaming and aggressive dry-heaving outside the studio window, welcome to the final broadcast."

Meet DJ Dusty. He used to host the graveyard shift, playing retro hits and taking calls from lonely truck drivers. But today, he’s the unofficial narrator of the end of days.
The world didn't end with a meteor, an alien invasion, or a nuclear war. It ended because someone decided to eat a suspiciously glowing, deeply unhygienic plate of street food that had been sitting in the sun for three business days. Patient Zero took one bite, and within hours, a catastrophic wave of weaponized, apocalyptic food poisoning swept across the globe, bringing society to its absolute, chaotic knees.

Armed with a dying microphone, a stash of stale instant noodles, and whatever frantic callers manage to patch through before their reception drops, DJ Dusty is tracking the "Dirty Apocalypse" in real-time. Tune in every week as he breaks down the sheer absurdity of the collapse, spins some tunes to keep the panic down, and interviews survivors who are dealing with the ultimate question: How do you rebuild civilization when everyone is fighting over the last roll of toilet paper?

It’s gross, it’s chaotic, and it is completely unhygienic. Welcome to the funniest end-of-the-world broadcast you’ll ever hear.

Subscribe now—before the power grid completely fails.

It seeks to contribute to a progressive and inclusive vision of global cooperation for the 21st century - one capable of countering current trends toward nationalist fragmentation and geopolitical rivalry.


This first podcast series focuses on the New International Economic Order, its historical origins, goals, achievements as well as shortcomings, and the lessons that it offers for contemporary debates. 

The Vienna Global Forum is the result of a close collaboration between ÖFSE (Austrian Foundation for Development Research), the International Political Economy Research Group at King’s College London and the Helsinki Centre for Global Political Economy.

title: Reinier van Lanschot - We Are Europe
author: Reinier van Lanschot
description:
We are living in a time of profound change. In Brussels, decisions are made every day that affect the lives of 450 million Europeans and shape the wider world. European cooperation is unique, far-reaching, and remarkably successful. Yet that story is rarely told in our national media or in everyday conversations.


In ‘We Are Europe’, Reinier van Lanschot takes readerson a journey across the continent. With historical perspective and vivid anecdotes, he reveals the real story behind European cooperation, its bold achievements and its serious shortcomings.


Van Lanschot does not shy away from Europe’s flaws. By examining today’s greatest challenges, he exposes the weaknesses of the current system and argues for the fundamental changes needed to safeguard our future. What emerges is a clear and hopeful vision for a renewed European project.


This book is both a wake-up call and an invitation: to recognise that Europe is not something distant, but something we all shape. Because the truth is simple – we are Europe.


Ingesproken door Eli Thorne

title: First America
author: Pushkin Industries
description:
Native people have been written out of the American story, but without us you don’t know what happened. This summer the United States will celebrate the 250-year anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. When you read the Declaration, you realize it is a list of complaints. The last entry, the climax in our founders’ reasons for rebellion against the Crown, is this: “He has excited… the merciless Indian Savages, whose known rule of warfare, is an undistinguished destruction of all ages, sexes and conditions.”
We have been told the Revolution was fought over taxation and representation. But what the founders were most angry about in our country’s most famous document was Indian affairs. How did generations of Americans miss this?
The first armed rebellion against the Crown was an attack on British forts that traded with tribes. When colonists threw tea into the Boston harbor, they dressed up like members of the Mohawk tribe—not for disguise, but because pretending to be Indian symbolized freedom and rebellion. The founding fathers’ first government failed because Indigenous nations were too powerful; war and diplomacy with Native people is why we have a central federal government.
Hosted and reported by Rebecca Nagle and  featuring leading Native historians,  First America unveils  how the founders’ treatment of Indigenous nations—and their resistance—shaped US democracy. The show  does not simply add another blemish to the image of the founding fathers, it reveals the real story of why the colonists rebelled, what kind of government they created, and, crucially, how our current political moment was 250 years in the making.

title: My Journey Through the Scriptures
author: Kevin Stone
description:
My name is Kevin Stone and I am turning my life over to Jesus Christ. I am nobody important, just an average guy. Okay, maybe even a little below average. I have had a life full of bad decisions, broken homes, families, and dreams. 

My mother had me in church as a young child, however, at the first oppertunity I left all that to live my life the way I wanted to. Which has led to three failed marriages, countless different jobs, drug use, unholy sexual encounters, a suicide attempt. violence, theviery, and honestly just a life not well lived. 

Thanks to the patient love and mercy of God, I am reunited with my Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ. With a deep longing to speak about God, the bible, and Jesus Christ this podcast begins. I am not a preacher, a teacher, or a biblical scholar. I am just someone who wants to learn and live the scriptures, and eventually go home with the Lord.

description: 🎙️ The Arts Amplified is a podcast about the messy middle of building a creative life. Hosted by opera mom, creative director, educator, nonprofit founder, and creative strategist Jenny Madruga Chavez, each episode explores artistry, storytelling, visibility, community, and the human side of creating meaningful work. Through honest conversations, practical ideas, and real-world experience, The Arts Amplified helps artists, creatives, and arts leaders build connection—not just content.

title: LifeLessons: Wisdom from People Who Lived Past 100
author: Erik Hensel
description:
Henry is 102 and still plays tennis three times a week. Enid is 101 and sharper than most people half her age.
What can we learn from those who’ve lived the longest?
LifeLessons is a podcast of raw, unfiltered conversations with centenarians, diving into life’s biggest questions, deepest regrets, and most valuable lessons after 100+ years of living.
From the creators of the viral LifeLessons film, featured by The New York Times and Good Morning America.

title: Live with Certainty
author: Dee
description: You're not in control anyway.  How to live without fear of anything in this world and create peace in your life and those around you.

title: THE COACHES ROWE
author: THE COACHES ROWE
description: The Coaches Rowe brings together coaches, leaders, athletes, parents, and performance professionals from across the country to share the lessons that shape successful teams, athletes, and lives. Hosted by Jacob Rowe, retired U.S. Army Master Sergeant, combat veteran, strength and conditioning coach, and leadership mentor, each episode explores leadership, culture, athletic performance, parenting, resilience, and personal growth. Pull up a chair and take your seat at The Coaches Rowe.

title: STIR
author: Aginto: A Digital Agency
description: STIR is what happens when entrepreneurs share their journey, insights, and mindset- giving us all a glimpse into what powers the people behind the businesses lifting our economy.

title: Unbroken Wings: The Podcast
author: N’Yirah
description:
Welcome to The Sanctuary, the home of Unbroken Wings: The Podcast.
Hosted by N'Yirah, this is a safe space for honest conversations about relationships, healing, faith, self-growth, and life's unexpected turns. Through authentic stories, meaningful discussions, and practical encouragement, you'll be reminded that your past doesn't define your future.
No judgment. No pretending. Just real conversations and real healing.
Because even with broken moments, your wings remain unbroken. 💛

title: The Evolution Podcast with Samantha Walker
author: Samantha Walker
description:
What happens when life asks you to reinvent yourself?


After 27 years as a nurse and healthcare leader, I made the decision to walk away from the security of a career I loved and bet on myself. Now, I’m building a business—and a life—that reflects who I truly am.

I’m Samantha Walker, nurse, entrepreneur, educator, and founder of Evolve Wellness & Aesthetics. Each week, we’ll have honest conversations about courage, reinvention, entrepreneurship, confidence, wellness, leadership, and navigating life’s biggest transitions.


Whether you’re chasing a dream, starting over, rebuilding after loss, or simply becoming the person you’re meant to be, this podcast is here to remind you that growth isn’t about becoming someone different—it’s about becoming more of who you’ve always been.


Welcome to The Evolution Podcast. Let’s keep evolving.

title: The Dignity Room
author: Eva Sillaber
description:
You're not ungrateful. You're awake. The Dignity Room is a podcast for high-achieving women in midlife who built the life they were told to want, and quietly feel empty inside it.
Each week, we name the things you've never said out loud: the perfect life that feels hollow, the exhaustion that isn't failure, the intuition you stopped trusting, and the honest truth about money and freedom. No hype, no five-step fixes, no "manifest your dream life." Just one woman to another, still on the road herself.
This isn't about fixing your life. It's about trusting yourself inside it again.

title: Act of Intelligence
author: Ajay Medury, Andrew Sierota
description: Act of Intelligence is a podcast where a software engineer (Ajay Medury) and systems engineer (Andrew Sierota) trade honest, in-the-trenches notes on building real things with AI — the tools, the workflows, and the philosophy — to figure out which old engineering wisdom still holds and which new instincts to trust.

title: The Process | presented by Kyle Anthony
author: Kyle
description: The Process exists to share the process I’m going through to become who God created me to be. Topics discussed will include personal life experiences, lessons, and practical takeaways. All of which will be rooted in transparency, truth, and authenticity.

title: The Siffy Scoop
author: Haley & George
description: Welcome to the Siffy Scoop! We're Haley & George, aka "The Siffy's". We love watching new shows and are looking to discuss them with people. Feel free to hop in the comments and tell us how right or wrong our takes are!

title: Brand Currency
author: Charles & Jonsi McGill
description:
Brand Currency is the podcast where branding actually makes sense.
Hosted by Charles and Jonsi McGill, this show breaks down how to build a brand that not only looks good, but works. Whether you're just starting out or scaling up, we talk design, messaging, confidence, and strategy in a way that’s clear, honest, and real. No fluff. No gimmicks. Just real conversations about the moves that grow brands and the mindset it takes to lead them.

title: Randomly Speaking with Kanu and Lumi
author: Kanu & Lumi
description: Randomly Speaking with Kanu and Lumi is a conversational podcast where Kanu and Lumi discuss life, culture, relationships, personal growth, current events, and the unexpected topics that come up when friends have honest conversations. Unfiltered and relatable conversations, always worth listening to.

title: the graceinletters podcast
author: Neeba Abraham
description: Welcome to the Graceinletters Podcast! I'm so honoured you're here! Life can be busy and sometimes it's just hard to read a blog so here's an audio version for you so you don't miss out. You can always read all my blogs at graceinletters.ca!

title: DLF on Demand
author: Caleb Niemi, Dynasty League Football, Addison Hayes
description: DLF on Demand is the official audio companion to the Dynasty League Football YouTube channel. Join Addison Hayes and Caleb Niemi year-round for rookie evaluations through The Marker System, actionable trade advice, and championship-winning strategies. It’s just the direct audio from our latest video content, delivered straight to your favorite podcast app so you can stay ahead of your league on your own schedule.

title: Know Thy Soul
author: Jennifer Maura DePascale
description:
Know Thy Soul is a podcast about the journey home to ourselves, to God, and to the wisdom waiting to be remembered.

 

Hosted by Jennifer Maura DePascale, each episode draws from myth, fairy tale, scripture, symbolism, ancient spiritual traditions, personal reflection, and conversations to uncover the timeless patterns woven through the world's great stories and spiritual traditions, and what they reveal about our lives today.

 

Together, we'll explore questions of meaning, healing, consciousness, relationship, and our direct connection to the divine. Through ancient stories and modern insights, we'll discover what these enduring patterns can teach us about ourselves, our world, and the journey from exile to wholeness.

 

Join us as we explore the lifelong journey of knowing thy soul.

title: Nugget for a Nugget
author: Bill Cunningham
description:
Nugget for a Nugget is built on one simple trade - chicken nuggets for nuggets of wisdom! 
Host Bill approaches strangers from all walks of life... some conversations are hilarious, others unexpectedly heartfelt, and some go completely off the rails! Every episode offers a glimpse into the thoughts, stories, regrets and little truths of ordinary people you’d usually walk straight past. 
The podcast also features special guests and friends of the podcast, all swapping stories, life lessons and unexpected truths. Turns out, some of the best wisdom in life comes from a humble box of nuggets!

title: Every Sound There Is: Exploring The Beatles’ Songs
author: David Pannell
description: The Beatles released 208 songs through EMI between 1962 and 1970. This podcast looks in detail at the stories behind each song, their composition, their arrangement, their recording, their lyrics, and so on.

title: The Couple Next Door – Old Time Radio Comedy (OTR)
description:
🎧 Welcome to The Couple Next Door, a delightful Old Time Radio comedy series that brings laughter and light-heartedness to your listening experience. This charming program, originating from the golden age of radio, features the humorous escapades of a suburban couple navigating the ups and downs of daily life.

📻 Each episode presents a unique scenario filled with witty banter, relatable situations, and comedic misunderstandings that resonate with audiences. Expect to hear tales of domestic life, quirky neighbors, and the amusing challenges that arise in a close-knit community. The Couple Next Door captures the essence of classic radio comedy with its engaging dialogue and memorable characters.

🎭 Collectors and enthusiasts of vintage radio will appreciate the nostalgic charm and timeless humor of this series. Fans of similar genres such as The Jack Benny Program and Fibber McGee and Molly will find much to enjoy in these delightful broadcasts. The Couple Next Door remains a notable entry in the OTR comedy landscape, offering full-length episodes that continue to entertain listeners today.

🔔 Tune in for more episodes of The Couple Next Door, classic Old Time Radio comedies, and curated vintage radio dramas from Heritage Radio Vault.

Delving into the unspoken truths about Real Estate with top-producing Realtors, Brokers, Attorneys, Loan officers, Title Agents, etc.

title: Trivia Crush - Trivia Quizzes
author: Main Street Media
description: Welcome to Trivia Crush, the podcast where knowledge meets competition in an exciting game show format. Each episode serves fast-paced questions across pop culture, history, geography and other great topics. Play along, compete, and see if you can crush the competition.

title: The Real with Dr. Wendi
author: Wendi
description:
The Real with Dr. Wendi is a practical, hope-filled podcast hosted by Dr. Wendi Watson, LCSW, Licensed Clinical Addiction Specialist, nonprofit leader, and university professor.

Real conversations on youth mental health, substance use prevention, community resources, and expert strategies for parents, caregivers, youth, and professionals.

Every week, Dr. Wendi delivers actionable tools, prevention strategies, powerful interviews with experts, and real stories that help families and communities navigate anxiety, depression, addiction risks, and the challenges facing today’s youth. Whether you’re a parent worried about your teen’s silence, a caregiver seeking community programs, or a professional supporting families, this podcast gives you the education, resources, and hope you need.

No fluff. No judgment. Just real solutions grounded in clinical expertise and community experience.

New episodes every week.
Subscribe now and join thousands of parents and professionals building stronger, healthier futures for our youth.

🎧 Available on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, YouTube, and everywhere else.

#YouthMentalHealth #TeenAnxiety #SubstancePrevention #ParentingTips #MentalHealthForParents #AddictionPrevention #YouthResources #DrWendiWatson #TheRealPodcast

title: The Marriage – Old Time Radio Comedy (OTR)
description:
🎧 Welcome to The Marriage, a classic Old Time Radio comedy series centered on married life, domestic situations, and the everyday humor of relationships. 📻 Episodes typically focus on husbands and wives, family routines, social situations, and the small conflicts that create lighthearted radio comedy. The series reflects the sitcom-style tradition of golden age broadcasting, with relatable domestic humor and warm character interaction.

title: The Misadventures of Si & Elmer – Old Time Radio Comedy (OTR)
description:
🎧 Welcome to The Misadventures of Si & Elmer, a delightful comedy series from the golden age of radio. title: Letters from Hollowmere
author: The Hollowmere Postman
description:
Bedtime stories for adults, told as letters from a man who's just moved to a small village called Hollowmere. He writes home each week — the inn, the square, the gentle strangenesses of a place he doesn't yet understand.
Slow stories, one warm British voice, about an hour. No jump scares, no music that builds — made to be drifted through. Best with headphones, lamps low. If you fall asleep before he signs off, that's the idea.
Narration created with ElevenLabs voice synthesis. Words written by a human.

title: The Naked Vicar Show – Old Time Radio Comedy (OTR)
description:
🎧 Welcome to The Naked Vicar Show, a classic Old Time Radio comedy series that brings to life the humorous antics of a fictional parish and its eccentric congregation. Set against the backdrop of the golden age of radio, this show captures the essence of British humor through its satirical take on everyday church life and the quirky characters that inhabit it.

📻 Each episode features the vicar and his delightful congregation, engaging in witty banter and comedic situations that reflect the absurdities of parish life. Expect a delightful mix of character-driven comedy and sharp satire, making it a staple for fans of vintage radio humor.

🎭 The Naked Vicar Show is celebrated for its clever writing and memorable performances by various radio performers, ensuring a delightful listening experience for collectors and enthusiasts of classic comedy. Its charm and wit resonate with those who appreciate the art of radio storytelling and character-driven narratives.

🔎 Join us for full-length broadcasts of this vintage comedy series, perfect for fans of classic comedy, OTR comedy, and radio humor. Discover the engaging world of The Naked Vicar Show and enjoy the timeless humor that has entertained audiences for generations.

title: The Pacific Story – Old Time Radio Documentary (OTR)
description:
🎧 The Pacific Story is a classic Old Time Radio documentary series focused on the Pacific region, World War II history, international affairs, and wartime analysis. This vintage program presents informative radio storytelling from the 1940s, using narration, dramatized segments, and historical context to examine events and issues connected to the Pacific theater.

📻 Episodes typically explore military, political, cultural, and strategic subjects rather than comedy sketches or fictional island stories. The series reflects the documentary style of golden age radio, offering listeners a period view of wartime developments, regional history, and public-interest broadcasting.

🎭 The Pacific Story is a strong choice for collectors and OTR listeners interested in wartime radio, historical documentaries, World War II broadcasts, Pacific theater history, and preserved educational radio programs. Its appeal comes from its serious documentary tone and its connection to the radio journalism of the era.

🔎 Tune in for full-length episodes of The Pacific Story, a preserved Old Time Radio documentary series featuring Pacific history, wartime analysis, historical narration, and classic 1940s radio broadcasting.

📻 Episodes typically follow space-age adventures, scientific imagination, villains, discoveries, and dramatic situations in outer space or futuristic environments. The series should be presented as science fiction adventure, not as a documentary or astronomy lecture program.

🎭 The Planet Man is a strong choice for collectors and OTR listeners who enjoy classic science fiction radio, space adventure, juvenile serials, futuristic drama, and preserved vintage broadcasts. It fits naturally beside programs such as Space Patrol, Tom Corbett, Dimension X, and X Minus One.

🔎 Tune in for full-length episodes of The Planet Man, a preserved Old Time Radio science fiction series featuring space adventure, futuristic storytelling, classic radio heroes, and golden age sci-fi entertainment.

📻 Episodes typically feature police cases, investigations, suspects, witnesses, and the steady process of solving crimes. The series should be presented as police drama and crime radio, not science fiction or futuristic adventure.

🎭 The Queen’s Men is a good choice for collectors and OTR listeners who enjoy classic police drama, detective radio, crime investigations, procedural storytelling, and preserved vintage broadcasts. It fits naturally beside programs such as Dragnet, Broadway Is My Beat, Gang Busters, and The Line Up.

🔎 Tune in for full-length episodes of The Queen’s Men, a preserved Old Time Radio police drama series featuring crime cases, law enforcement stories, detective work, and classic golden age radio entertainment.

🎧 Welcome to The Secrets of Scotland Yard, a classic Old Time Radio detective series that delves into the thrilling world of crime investigations and the relentless pursuit of justice. Set against the backdrop of London's Scotland Yard, this vintage radio drama captures the essence of the golden age of radio with its engaging narratives and compelling characters.

📻 Each episode features a cast of classic radio performers portraying detectives and investigators as they unravel complex cases filled with intrigue and suspense. The series showcases the meticulous work of law enforcement, highlighting the challenges faced by those who seek to uphold the law in a city rife with crime.

🎭 The Secrets of Scotland Yard appeals to collectors and enthusiasts of vintage radio, offering a glimpse into the detective genre's rich history. Fans of similar shows such as Dragnet, The Adventures of Philip Marlowe, and The Shadow will find much to enjoy in this series, which remains a notable entry in the realm of Old Time Radio detective dramas.

🔎 Join us as we explore full-length broadcasts of this captivating series, featuring classic crime investigations and the timeless allure of detective storytelling.

title: What’s the Point? with Richard Denniss
author: The Australia Institute
description:
What’s the Point? with Richard Denniss cuts through the spin to explain the political and economic decisions shaping the lives of everyday Australians.
Richard connects the dots between policy and politics with sharp analysis. By getting straight to the point, Richard helps listeners understand how democracy works and why it matters.
Presented by the Australia Institute.

title: Evalve Out Loud

description:
Evalve Out Loud is a podcast for educators who know the system is not working and are curious about what comes next.
Hosted by Chris, the show features honest stories, calm reflections, and real conversations about education, leadership, special education, and AI. Some episodes are short solo thoughts. Others are conversations with educators and builders.

description:

When he escapes, he is caught up in his thirst for revenge against his accuser, Messala, and his search for the Messiah of his people, the King who is to come. As Judah learns more about this King, however, he begins to realize that the kingdom he is searching for may not be found in what he can see and the revenge he is seeking may not be found in the way that he expects. Summary by Esther ben Simonides

title: Belton Estate, The by Anthony Trollope (1815 - 1882)
description:
Clara Amedroz is the virtuous, intelligent, and quick-witted heroine of this novel. Like all women of her time, she has few options other than to marry. She is lucky enough to have two eligible suitors, and chooses the more urbane and worldly of the two. Alas, however, she realizes fairly quickly that Captain Aylmer is not a nice person. Throughout much of the novel we find her trying hard not to recognize that Will Belton - the suitor she rejected, and who still loves and wants to marry her - is.

As in all of Trollope's novels, the sub-plots are at least as engaging as the main story: here, we find Clara associated with, and ultimately for some time dependent on, Mr. and Mrs. Askerton, who - having perforce lived together for some time before they were married - are social outcasts. Clara is courageous enough to remain loyal to these friends, knowing that thus she, too, risks social condemnation and reduces her value on the marital market-place.

She becomes wiser, more generous, and more forgiving as her outlook on the world matures in various trials-by fire: the experiences she endures as a result of her mistaken allegiance to Captain Aylmer; her friendship with the Askertons; and the malicious gossip and social ostracization attendant on her loyalty to them. The question that will decide her ultimate happiness is whether she can be as generous and forgiving of herself. (Summary by Kirsten Wever)

title: Belshazzar by Georg Friedrich Händel (1685 - 1759) and Charles Jennens (1700 - 1773)

The story is taken from the Book of Daniel in the Old Testament and tells the tale of Belshazzar's Feast and "the writing on the wall" which only Daniel can interpret and which foretells the downfall of Belshazzar.

(Summary by Alan Mapstone)

Paragot leads Asticot on various adventures through 19th century Europe, eventually arriving in Paris. Here Paragot’s dark romantic past begins to catch up with him. (Summary by Simon Evers)

title: Ligature Valley
author: Kaleb Dean
description:
Ligature Valley is a long-form type design critique show for designers who want to think deeper than the algorithm. Each episode breaks down new and notable typefaces—retail fonts, brand commissions, and experimental releases—through a structured rubric that scores originality, craft, usability, and cultural impact.

Listen for free wherever you get your podcasts—or subscribe at ligaturevalley.com for full video versions, extended commentary, and all the visuals you’d expect from a show about type design.

Ad-free, thoughtful, and designed to last.

title: Bells of San Juan, The by  Jackson Gregory (1882 - 1943)
description: Rod Norton is a lawman in a land where bandits and criminals make their own rules. Risking his life for justice and a future with the woman he loves, mortal danger awaits. For Norton and those in peril, the Bells of San Juan will chime. (Summary by Betty M.)

title: Bells of Is, The by Frederick Brotherton Meyer (1847 - 1929)
description:
"It has always seemed to me, amid submerged masses, deep down at the bottom of the ocean of human life, there are yearnings and desires for a better life, that ring sadly and perpetually. It has been the aim of my life to listen for these, and where I have detected them, to present the only answer -- the love of God in Jesus Christ our Lord.

Some of the ways in which I have sought to do this during my Leicester life are narrated in this book, which serves to show what may be done in this direction amid the cares of a busy pastorate." (from the Preface)

title: Wholeness Unlocked
author: Karlonty
description: You don't have to fake the smile anymore. Wholeness Unlocked is the podcast for women of faith tired of performing okay while quietly carrying pain and trauma. Hosted by Karlonty L. Wallace — Self-Help Author, Trauma-Informed Life Coach, and Faith-Based Healing Specialist — each episode brings NIV scripture, real talk, and healing strategy. No matter your background or nationality, there is room for you here. You are enough. You have purpose. Let's walk in it.

title: Bells and Other Poems, The by Edgar Allan Poe (1809 - 1849)
description: This is a collection of the most famous poems by Edgar Allan Poe. It includes all of his most famous poems, such as the Bells and Annabel Lee, but also some minor and  less well-known poems. Readers may wish to refer to the online text for 28 beautiful colour illustrations by Edmund Dulac. - Summary by Carolin

title: Bells, The by Edgar Allan Poe (1809 - 1849)
description:
LibriVox readers bring you 18 recordings of "The Bells." This was the Fortnightly Poetry selection for January 30 to February 13, 2011.

"The Bells" is a heavily onomatopoeic poem by Edgar Allan Poe which was not published until after his death in 1849. It is perhaps best known for the diacopic repetition of the word "bells." The poem has four parts to it; each part becomes darker and darker as the poem progresses from "the jingling and the tinkling" of the bells in part 1 to the "moaning and the groaning" of the bells in part 4. (From Wikipedia)

title: The Native Sound Podcast
author: Johnnie LeAundra Reed
description:
Welcome to The Native Sound Podcast, your premier destination for authentic conversations at the intersection of Black culture, breaking news, untold history, and the pulse of the music industry. Hosted by veteran songwriter and digital creator Native, this podcast bridges the gap between the foundational roots of our culture and the modern digital age.Drawing on decades of hands-on music industry grit—from early street team hustles and A&R work in New Orleans to penning songs since 1992—Native brings a raw, insider’s perspective to the mic. Combined with a sharp eye as a YouTube content creator and social media influencer, The Native Sound doesn't just report on the culture; it analyzes, honors, and pushes it forward. Whether we are dissecting historical milestones, unpacking the latest headlines, or exploring the business of music, this is where the culture speaks. Subscribe and join the tribe.

Become a supporter of this podcast: https://www.spreaker.com/podcast/the-native-sound-podcast--6715066/support.

title: Bellarion the Fortunate: a romance by Rafael Sabatini (1875 - 1950)
description:
This novel, in three parts, follows the adventures of a young man, Bellarion, after he leaves the safety of the monastery that had nurtured him as an orphan waif. As young man of striking good looks, intelligence, wit, and unswerving loyalty, Bellarion's first five years in the world prove full of adventure, danger and romance. His life is intertwined with powerful Regents, Marquis, Dukes and Princesses with rogues, thieves, and pretenders amongst their midst. His strategic brilliance ensures him fame on the battlefields of 15th century Northern Italy but will his efforts avail him in this new and stunning world? More especially will he ever win the heart of the skeptical Princess Valeria? The novel intertwines real historical figures and events with fictional embellishments to craft a rapidly moving tale of adventure, strategy and philosophy on the motivations of human ambition, vanity and folly..
(Summary by KiwiReader24)

title: Bell Upon Organ by George MacDonald (1824 - 1905)
description: George MacDonald (10 December 1824 – 18 September 1905) was a Scottish author, poet, and Christian minister. He was a pioneering figure in the field of fantasy literature and the mentor of fellow writer Lewis Carroll. His writings have been cited as a major literary influence by many notable authors including W. H. Auden, C. S. Lewis, J. R. R. Tolkien, Walter de la Mare, E. Nesbit and Madeleine L'Engle. (Summary form Wikipedia)

title: Belinda: An April Folly in Three Acts by A. A. Milne (1882 - 1956)

title: Meet Me at the Highland™
author: Gabrielle Wyatt
description:
Meet Me at the Highland™ is a podcast and sacred space centering Black women's leadership, imagination, and legacy. Hosted by Gabrielle Wyatt, founder of The Highland Project, it offers intimate, intergenerational conversations with changemakers, artists, and cultural architects reimagining what it means to thrive. It's a rhythm of rest, reflection, and radical dreaming, where we remember who we are, reclaim our joy, and begin again. Season Two, Seeding, is unfolding now, with Highland Leaders like Brittany Packnett Cunningham, Dr. Topeka K. Sam, and Brea Baker. Each episode arrives with a love letter, journaling prompts, and reflections that invite deeper introspection. This is your invitation. To lead. To rest. To imagine forward. To meet us at the Highland.

meetmeatthehighland.substack.com

title: what she said
author: Angie Birky
description: What She Said is a gathering place for women and their stories. Each episode features real conversations about love, friendship, motherhood, reinvention, aging, work, purpose, loss, joy, and everything in between. Host Angie Birky sits down with women from all walks of life to explore the lessons, challenges, and moments that have shaped who they are—and who they’re becoming.

title: Belinda by Maria Edgeworth (1768 - 1849)
description:
When Belinda was published in 1801, it became both controversial and popular. Controversial because of the inter-racial marriage presented in the novel, and popular because it's a very good comedy of manners, like Evelina by Fanny Burney. Belinda, like Evelina, is a soft and loving girl of 17, is coming to London with her aunt who directs her action in order to make sure that she'll find a good match. But what will happen if Belinda will fall in love? Will Clarence Hervey, the man she loves, be able to marry her? It seems almost impossible, as he is secretly bringing up another woman to be a perfect wife to him and now, in all honor, he thinks he must marry her.

These social novels about young women trying to find good husbands were admired by Jane Austen who referred to Belinda, among other novels, in her own novel Northanger Abbey: “'And what are you reading, Miss — ?' 'Oh! It is only a novel!' replies the young lady, while she lays down her book with affected indifference, or momentary shame. 'It is only Cecilia, or Camilla, or Belinda'; or, in short, only some work in which the greatest powers of the mind are displayed, in which the most thorough knowledge of human nature, the happiest delineation of its varieties, the liveliest effusions of wit and humour, are conveyed to the world in the best–chosen language." (Summary by Stav Nisser)

title: Believe Me, if All Those Endearing Young Charms by Thomas Moore (1779 - 1852)
description: LibriVox volunteers bring you 17 recordings of Believe Me, If All Those Endearing Young Charms by Thomas Moore. This was the Weekly Poetry project for February 14th, 2010.

description: This story is based upon the experiences of two Belgian refugees in World War I. When their parents are marched of by Germans, Jan and Marie are left alone. Now they, along with their dog, have to find their parents! (Summary by ashleighjane)

title: Belfry Pigeon, The by Nathaniel Parker Willis (1806 - 1867)
description: Nathaniel Parker Willis, also known as N. P. Willis, was an American author, poet and editor who worked with several notable American writers including Edgar Allan Poe and Henry Wadsworth Longfellow. He became the highest-paid magazine writer of his day. For a time, he was the employer of former slave and future writer Harriet Jacobs. - Summary by Wikipedia

From the 1933 bankruptcy to the 2033 Synthesis, we dissect how Dark Organizations are liquidating the American Republic. Discover the four pillars of the final capture: Perishable Currency, Geofenced Reality, and the end of due process.

title: Bel Ami, or The History of a Scoundrel by Guy de Maupassant (1850 - 1893)
description:
“He had faith in his good fortune, in that power of attraction which he felt within him - a power so irresistible that all women yielded to it.”

Though firmly set in 1880s Paris, Maupassant's gripping story of an amoral journalist on the make could, with only slight modifications of detail, be updated to the 1960s, to the Reagan-Thatcher years, or maybe to the present day. Anti-hero Georges Duroy is a down-at-heel ex-soldier of no particular talent. Good-looking but somewhat lacking in self-confidence, he discovers an ability to control and exploit women - whereupon his career in journalism takes off, fuelled by the corruption of colleagues and government arrivistes. He may be a provincial Don Juan, but he is neither accident-prone nor heading for a fall...

A Hollywood screen adaptation is in preparation at the time of recording.
(Summary by Martin Geeson)

description: Wealth Made Simple is the podcast that breaks down the strategies the wealthy use to build, protect, and multiply their money—without the confusing jargon. Hosted by entrepreneur, tax strategist, and Enrolled Agent Karlton Dennis, each episode delivers practical insights on taxes, investing, real estate, business, entrepreneurship, AI, and personal finance to help you keep more of what you earn and create lasting wealth. Through conversations with successful entrepreneurs, investors, and industry experts, you'll learn the frameworks, habits, and financial strategies that separate those who build wealth from those who simply earn an income. Whether you're growing a business, investing for the future, or looking to make smarter financial decisions, Wealth Made Simple gives you actionable advice you can implement right away.

description:
The Pod Cart is the only podcast filmed from inside a golf cart on Florida's finest courses.

Host Darren Dowling of Beyond Realty in Sarasota brings the conversation out of the studio and onto the fairway. No scripts, no stiff intros, no fluorescent lights. Just an honest conversation that unfolds naturally between the first tee and the 19th hole.

Recorded at private clubs and public courses across Sarasota, Lakewood Ranch, and the Gulf Coast, with occasional travel episodes from across the state of Florida.

If you love a good conversation, a good golf course, and an honest take on business and life in the Sunshine State, this is your show.

New episodes every other Tuesday. Subscribe wherever you get your podcasts.

Each episode features thoughtful conversations with pastors, ministry leaders, educators, healthcare professionals, and church members who are passionate about advancing God’s kingdom. Together we explore Scripture, discipleship, evangelism, stewardship, leadership, education, health ministry, and the stories of how God is working throughout Oklahoma and beyond.
Whether you’re looking for biblical encouragement, practical ministry insights, leadership development, or updates on conference initiatives, Kingdom Conversations is designed to inform, inspire, and equip you to participate more fully in Christ’s mission.
Join us as we celebrate what God is doing, discuss the challenges facing the church, and discover how every believer has a role in building God’s kingdom.

title: Wilco360
author: Optimus Media Group
description:
Wilco 360 is a podcast about Williamson County, the issues, the opportunities, and the people behind the decisions shaping one of the fastest growing regions in Texas.


Hosts Mike Payne and Camy Reynolds have spent more than a decade building relationships with the people steering the county, from elected officials and business leaders to developers and local voices. Those relationships give them a vantage point most people don't have, and they bring it to every episode.


From water and infrastructure to growth, economic development, transportation, and local politics, Wilco 360 breaks down what's really happening across the county, the stuff that shapes life here but rarely gets explained well. Each episode also features recurring segments like Wilco Wins, a look at something good happening locally, and Fact or Fiction, where Mike and Camy take on a local hot topic and call it straight.


If you live, work, or build in Williamson County, this is your seat at the table.


Subscribe and follow along at https://wilco360.live

title: Fortunes and Misfortunes of the Famous Moll Flanders
description: Defoe wrote this novel after his work as a journalist and pamphleteer. By 1722, Defoe had become recognized as a novelist, with the success of Robinson Crusoe in 1719. His political work was tapering off at this point, due to the fall of both Whig and Tory party leaders with whom he had been associated. - Defoe's Whig views are nevertheless evident in the story of Moll. The full title of the novel tells part of its story: "The Fortunes and Misfortunes of the Famous Moll Flanders, Etc. Who was born in Newgate, and during a life of continu'd Variety for Threescore Years, besides her Childhood, was Twelve Year a Whore, five times a Wife (whereof once to her own brother), Twelve Year a Thief, Eight Year a Transported Felon in Virginia, at last grew Rich, liv'd Honest and died a Penitent. Written from her own Memorandums." (Summary from Wikipedia)

title: Horror Stories 911
description:
Horror Stories 911 is the podcast for those who crave emergency-level fear, terrifying stories, and chilling encounters that feel impossible to escape. If you find yourself searching for scary stories, creepy experiences, paranormal events, haunted places, and horror podcasts that keep your heart racing, this is where fear becomes urgent.

Every episode of Horror Stories 911 pulls you into dark and disturbing stories filled with suspense, panic, and the kind of tension that feels like a call made too late. This is not just about horror—it’s about desperate moments, strange warnings, unexplained sounds, terrifying encounters, and the chilling realization that something is wrong before anyone can help. You will hear stories of shadow figures, haunted homes, late-night calls, missing people, cursed locations, strange voices, and events that feel dangerously close to real life.

Horror Stories 911 delivers a powerful mix of scary stories, paranormal horror, creepy mysteries, and unsettling real-life inspired tales crafted to feel intense, immersive, and impossible to pause. Designed for horror fans who love suspenseful podcasts, dark storytelling, supernatural fear, and disturbing narratives, each episode pulls you deeper into a world where every second feels critical.

If you are obsessed with horror podcasts, scary stories, creepy encounters, and paranormal experiences that stay with you long after the episode ends, Horror Stories 911 will quickly become part of your nightly routine. These are the stories that make your pulse rise, your room feel darker, and every strange sound feel like a warning.

Because once you start listening to Horror Stories 911… fear is already on the line.

title: Lifes Apologies for All People by Carolann Murray
description: Step into a transformative journey of redemption and healing with this debut season of Lifes Apologies for All People, hosted by Carolann Murray. Through a powerful blend of raw human narratives and faith-guided insights, you will transition from the heavy chains of past regrets and unresolved conflict into a state of profound inner peace. By exploring the depths of accountability and grace, this season guides you to unlock the life-altering power of apologies to finally mend your most fractured relationships.  Listeners will walk away with a practical blueprint for identifying and breaking generational cycles, navigating complex family dynamics, and establishing healthy boundaries. You will master the essential framework of taking full ownership of your mistakes, transforming defensive reactions into constructive steps toward reconciliation. Furthermore, you will develop the spiritual tools of prayer and compassion needed to release bitterness, heal from injustices, and embrace a future completely liberated from shame.  This season is crafted specifically for anyone carrying the weight of unsaid apologies, enduring painful family rifts, or seeking to break free from the shadow of past mistakes. If you are yearning for a deeper connection with God and a practical pathway to forgive both yourself and others, this podcast is your safe haven. You are not defined by where you have been, and this series is here to light your path home to grace.

This is Unipkausivut Our Stories, a series produced by Pauktuutit Inuit Women of Canada featuring the experiences and insights of Inuit women from across Inuit Nunangat. Learn how Inuit women are overcoming gender inequities, advocating for participation in non-traditional roles, strengthening the economic and mental well-being of their communities, and empowering Inuit youth to carry traditional knowledge into the future.
